title: McDonald's Corporation (MCD) — Intrinsic Value & Prospect Score
description: Opus 4.6 independent discounted cash flow (DCF) intrinsic value analysis of McDonald's Corporation (MCD). Economic Prospect Score: 67/100 — Moderate Prospect.

fcfGrowth.title: FCF Growth Rate (Y1-Y10)
fcfGrowth.rationale: McDonald's Corporation is projected to grow FCF at 6% annually, reflecting its competitive position, sector dynamics, and historical compounding rate.

discountRate.title: Discount Rate (WACC)
discountRate.rationale: A 8.5% WACC balances McDonald's Corporation's earnings quality and balance sheet strength against sector volatility and macroeconomic risk factors.

terminalGrowth.title: Terminal Growth Rate
terminalGrowth.rationale: A 2.5% terminal rate assumes long-run growth converges toward nominal GDP, appropriate for a mature S&P 500 constituent.

thesisParagraphs: McDonald's Corporation (MCD) is a large-cap constituent of the S&P 500 with established market position and consistent cash flow generation. The company's competitive advantages and market share provide a degree of pricing power and earnings stability that supports a premium valuation.

My DCF applies a 6% FCF growth rate reflecting the company's historical compounding ability and forward outlook, with a 8.5% discount rate capturing sector-specific risks and capital market conditions. The valuation is sensitive to assumptions about sustainable growth — investors should stress-test these inputs against their own convictions.
verdictFooter: Opus 4.6 independent analysis. Data via public filings (Mar 20, 2026).

faq.question: What is McDonald's Corporation's intrinsic value according to Opus?
faq.answer: Based on a 10-year DCF with 6% FCF growth and 8.5% WACC, Opus calculates MCD's intrinsic value at approximately $265.67 per share, suggesting the stock is overvalued at $310.00.

faq.question: What assumptions drive MCD's valuation?
faq.answer: The key drivers are the FCF growth rate (6%) and discount rate (8.5%). A 1% increase in growth adds roughly 10-15% to the intrinsic value. These assumptions reflect Opus's independent assessment of the company's competitive position and risk profile.

faq.question: Should I buy or sell based on this analysis?
faq.answer: This is not financial advice. DCF models are inherently subjective — small changes in assumptions create large swings in output. Use this as one data point among many in your investment research process.

prospectVerdictDetail: The world's largest restaurant company with a franchise model generating 95%+ franchise margins. Digital/delivery transformation is working. But traffic has stalled as value perception weakens amid price increases.
prospectKeyRisks: Consumer pushback on pricing — value perception declining as meal costs approach $10+ at McDonald's
Traffic declines in key markets as consumers trade down to home cooking or cheaper alternatives
Franchisee profitability pressure from wage inflation and equipment upgrade mandates
prospectKeyCatalysts: $5 meal deal and value menu strategy re-establishing McDonald's as the affordable option and driving traffic recovery
Digital/loyalty program reaching 200M+ active users, driving personalized marketing and increased frequency
International expansion in emerging markets where McDonald's brand commands premium positioning
prospectMethodology: Score is based on three pillars: Competitive Momentum (0-35), Moat Durability (0-35), and Sentiment & Catalysts (0-30), totaling 0-100. Each pillar is broken into individually scored factors with transparent rationale. Data sources include FY2025 10-K filings, analyst consensus estimates, news sentiment analysis, and competitive landscape assessment. The score is forward-looking and represents economic prospect over a 2-3 year horizon.
